one_line_thesis: Global water technology compounder (post-Evoqua four-segment platform); FY2025 FCF ~$3.73/sh; synthesis -0.3% at ~$130 → watch.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Xylem is a global water technology leader with durable utility relationships, specification-driven equipment brands, and a growing outsourced services platform built through the Evoqua combination. FY2025 free cash flow was approximately $3.73 per share on $910 million of free cash flow, and Q1 2026 revenue grew 2.7% to $2.125 billion with diluted earnings per share of $0.79 versus $0.69 a year earlier. At $130.00, base expected annual return is -0.3% per year, below our 15% hurdle. Durable competitive advantage (Munger moat) is stable: installed base, brand specification, and utility servi...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

timeline.label: Operating income moved +21.2% versus the prior period (1,009.0 to 1,223.0).
Q1 2026 declared dividend $0.43 per share (11% increase in dividends paid in 2025); signals continued cash return after Evoqua integration
Q1 2026 revenue $2.125B (+2.7% YoY); diluted EPS $0.79 vs $0.69; four-segment platform still compounding modestly
FY2025 revenue $9.035B (+5.5%); free cash flow ~$910M; long-term debt reduced to $1.408B from $1.978B; adjusted operating margin 17.8%
Acquired Vacom Systems LLC (measurement/water quality); expands Measurement and Control Solutions footprint
FY2024 revenue $8.562B; adjusted operating income $1.373B (16.0% margin); Evoqua integration margin gains visible year two post-merger
Board and governance refresh continued post-Evoqua; integration committees wind down as segment reporting stabilizes
First full year as four-segment water platform (Water Infrastructure, Applied Water, Measurement and Control, Water Solutions and Services)
Closed Evoqua Water Technologies merger — transformed Xylem into combined equipment + outsourced water services leader (~$2.5B WSS segment at scale)
Announced Evoqua merger agreement — all-stock combination to expand treatment and services exposure
Pre-merger Xylem revenue $5.522B; three-segment structure before Evoqua combination
Spun from ITT Corporation as standalone water pure-play (historical context for segment heritage: Flygt, Goulds, Sensus brands)

industry.structure: Global water technology oligopoly with four application-focused segments; utility and industrial customers value installed base, specification lock-in, and service contracts. Post-Evoqua, Xylem competes with Veolia, Suez, Pentair, and regional pump/treatment OEMs.
industry.share_shift: Xylem gained scale in outsourced water services via Evoqua (WSS segment $2.464B FY2025); smart metering (Sensus) faces AMI upgrade cycles; municipal infrastructure spending supports Water Infrastructure transport/treatment mix.
industry.trend: Aging water infrastructure, lead-pipe replacement, PFAS treatment, and digital metering drive demand; integration synergies and restructuring ($40–55M expected 2026) continue post-merger; services and recurring revenue mix rising.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Premium valuation embeds continued margin expansion and mid-single-digit owner-cash growth; any Measurement margin relapse or municipal capex pause forces multiple compression.
Measurement margin pressure: Operating margin 11.7% in 2025 versus 13.2% in 2024; smart-metering competition may persist (10-K segment note).
Integration and restructuring: $133 million restructuring/realignment in 2025; management guides $40 to $55 million for full-year 2026. Charges must convert to sustained margin, not recurring drag.
